questions.answer: Carbon dioxide (CO2) and other greenhouse gases

What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
questions.answer: An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by an external force (Law of Inertia)

questions.question: What is the formula for force in Newton's Second Law?
questions.answer: F = ma (Force equals mass times acceleration)

questions.question: According to Newton's Third Law, for every action there is an equal and opposite what?
What is the acceleration due to gravity on Earth (approximately)?
What is the SI unit of force?
What is kinetic energy?
questions.answer: The energy of motion (or energy possessed by a moving object)

questions.question: What is the formula for kinetic energy?
questions.answer: KE = ½mv² (half mass times velocity squared)

questions.question: What is potential energy?
questions.answer: Stored energy (or energy due to position or configuration)

questions.question: What is the law of conservation of energy?
questions.answer: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted from one form to another

questions.question: What is the SI unit of work and energy?
What is the name of our galaxy?
What is the closest star to Earth?
What is a light-year?
questions.answer: The distance light travels in one year (approximately 9.46 trillion kilometers)

questions.question: What is the largest moon of Saturn?
What is the name of the force that prevents stars from collapsing?
What is a supernova?
questions.answer: A massive stellar explosion that occurs at the end of a star's life

questions.question: What is the Kuiper Belt?
questions.answer: A region of the solar system beyond Neptune containing icy bodies and dwarf planets

questions.question: What is the Great Red Spot on Jupiter?
What is a black hole?
questions.answer: A region of spacetime with gravity so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape
